observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 are strictly observed to fast track and facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances by instructing the:

Members of the Inventory Committee to immediately prioritize the physical inventory of all PPEs of the LGU above their regular functions in the LGU;

The Inventory Committee to transfer the data in the working paper during the physical count in a standard Inventory Count Form (ICF) per PPE type, using the format in Annex A of this Circular, which shall be used as the basis in the preparation of the RPCPPE after the physical count; an prepare and submit the RPCPPE covering all the PPEs of the LGU.

The Property Unit to prepare and update Property records based on the results of the physical inventory and reconcile with accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the LGU's PPEs; and


The Property and Accounting Unit to undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that the PCs maintained by the Property Unit and the PPELCs maintained by the Accounting Unit are reconciled. The reconciliation to be completed within ten (10) days from the rendition of the RPCPPE by the Inventory Committee.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct:

Inventory Committee to:

Conduct actual physical count of the road network system per books;

Update the Physical Count Report on the Local Road Network and forward the report to the Municipal Treasurer for reconciliation, copy furnished COA.

The GSO/Municipal Treasurer to maintain and regularly update the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and

The Municipal Accountant to:

Maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and its components;

Reconcile the same to the result of the physical count at year end and to properly depreciate the account; and 

Properly disclose the road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements as at year-end in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to transfer the PPE from the TF to the GF books; and

Ensure that all PPEs subject for depreciation have been provided with depreciation expense and to create a new lapsing schedule for monitoring purposes.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the:

Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Accountant at the beginning of the year, a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. Also, at year-end to certify delinquencies remaining uncollected for all RPT delinquencies;

Municipal Accountant shall draw a JEV to record the debit to RPT Receivables/ SET Receivables and crediting to Deferred RPT/SET Income with corresponding schedule; and

Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Assessor’s Office, to undertake reconciliation of the balances RPTAR and per certified list of RPT with the RPT and SET Receivable account balances per books of accounts to come up with a more accurate and proper balances of RPT and SET Receivable accounts from 2023 and prior years.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:

Municipal Accountant with coordination of Municipal Treasurer to exert effort to collect these long outstanding receivables particularly the accounts with available reference documents by sending demand letters. Exhaust all remedies to collect and properly document efforts made and more effort to locate all related documents pertaining to the long outstanding receivables in order to establish their existence and validity;

Municipal Accountant to (i) develop accounting estimate for impairment of receivables based on the management’s knowledge and experience on the settlement of its receivables taking into account the age and collectability of the balances, identified bad accounts eligible for write-off and other historical data such as settlement/liquidation and collection experience; (ii) assess at the end of each year or each reporting date whether there is evidence that the receivable accounts are impaired; (iii) support the assessment, if possible, with evidence enumerated in paragraph 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005; (iv) calculate the allowance for impairment in accordance with the IPSAS and recognize the computed allowance in the books to reflect the net realizable value of the receivable accounts in the Financial Statements; and (v) submit to the Local Chief Executive the recommended list of eligible accounts for write-off; and 

Municipal Accountant through the Local Chief Executive (i) file a request for write-off of dormant accounts in accordance with the guidelines and procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 2023-008, if warranted, but continue to exert effort to collect the written-off accounts; and (ii) develop policies and guidelines outlining the methods and strategies to improve the collection and settlement of past due accounts and thereafter ensure strict adherence thereto to cleanse the books for the fair presentation of receivable accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Assessor to:

Proactively coordinate with the OPA to allow immediate scheduling and/or finalization of timelines on the planned province-wide assessment; and

Plan and implement the expected update of the Assessment Rolls as to the conditions of the real properties, its actual use, and the exemptions on RPT, if any.

We recommended and Management agreed to communicate with pertinent government agencies and request their performance indicators and incorporate it in their monitoring and evaluation functions and to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to set up the appropriate mechanism to collect comparable data and information on a regular basis and provide timely analysis of the information.

We recommended that Management instruct the IAS Officer to timely conduct an evaluation or appraisal of the ICS to determine if internal controls are well designed and properly implemented as required by the PGIAM by:

Appraising the adequacy of internal controls and documenting the same;

Conducting management audit, and the evaluation of the results of operations, focusing on the effectiveness of controls of operating systems and support services/units/systems and documenting the same; and

Preparing the various reports and documents presented in the manual.
We recommended and Management agreed to continue improve the internal control system of the LGU by addressing the remaining gaps presented.

We recommended and Management agreed to coordinate with the provider of the Accounting System and request the:

Performance of the appropriate trouble shooting procedures to correct the identified internal system issues and glitches in the Accounting System ensuring that the system contains all the necessary features and operates efficiently and as intended; and,
 
Continuous customer support be easily available whenever issues are presented.

We recommended and Management agreed to institutionalize the APCPI by instructing the BAC Secretariat to review the user manual for the preparation of the APCPI and to timely prepare and submit the report and prepare the 2021 to 2023 APCPI and prospectively the results for the Municipality.

We recommended that management require the Accounting Unit to exert extra effort to conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable, for authorized receivables, and that CA and fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending upon their nature and purpose. Otherwise, the same are subject to immediate refund by the liable persons.

Consider the requirements for write-off under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, which prescribes the guidelines and procedures in reconciling and cleaning the books of accounts of National Government Agencies (NGAs), Local Government Units (LGU), and Government Owned and/or Controlled Corporations (GOCCs) of dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated CA, and fund transfers.

We recommended that Management instruct:

The Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Local Road Network Property Card, Local Road Network Ledger Card, Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and the Report on Local Road Network; and

The Municipal Accountant to maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical count at year end and to properly depreciate the account, and to properly disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the LCE instruct:

The Inventory Committee to identify land/real properties owned by the Municipality and require the appraisal of the lots by a licensed appraiser for purposes of the land valuation; and

The Municipal Accountant considers application of the Revaluation Model as accounting policy on the Land account of the PPE, to come up with a fair presentation of the account balance.

We recommended that management:

Direct the Municipal General Services Officer to create an inventory committee, identify and specify PPEs used in the operation of the LEEs aside from the investment property;
 
Direct the Municipal Accountant to create and maintain a special account for each LEE in the general fund; and

Instruct the Local Finance Committee to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the proper reporting and monitoring of the revenue and expense of the respective LEEs concerned.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of year the list of all the on-going PPAs and those that are scheduled for implementation throughout the year as well as the quarterly monitoring report on the implementation of the PPAs of all funds. The prescribed form provided in the circular shall be used indicating in the remarks column the mode of procurement and specific status of the project such as on-going, for implementation, completed, suspended/ cancelled, terminated and for bidding

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:

Require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately;

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Cashier/Accountant submit the transaction documents: Disbursement Vouchers, Payrolls, Official Receipts, JEVs for adjustments, liquidations, etc. and their corresponding supporting documents, within the reglementary period to ensure their timely evaluation and that deficiencies and/or other observations will be also immediately corrected and/or adjusted.

That is, the personnel involved in the daily recording of transactions in the books of accounts shall turnover the receipts and the disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transactions to the Auditor within ten (10) days from the date of receipt of said documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended the following:

Municipal Accountant determine the valid claimants of the balances indicated and prepare subsidiary ledgers for each valid claimant. Balances with no valid claimants should be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and should be subject for appropriation in the next immediate budget period.

Municipal Accountant identify the reasons for the non-remittance of funds due to the Provincial Government and take up the necessary adjustments, as necessary; 

Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other in order to prevent future delays in computations and preparations of pertinent accounting documents to facilitate the prompt release and to release immediately any unreleased shares to the barangays concerned.

We recommended that the LSB Chairman to :

Meet at least monthly with the members of the LBS to address the issues in the education system in the public sector, and in coordinated efforts plan the allocation of the SEF largely on the priority projects enumerated under JC No. 01;

Stop the practice of paying from the SEF expenses which are not allowable; and

Include allocations from their SEF for the ECCD PPAs.

We recommended that the LGU comply with the pertinent requirements and regulations concerning the transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the SEF as required by DepEd, DBM, and DILG JC No. 01. Also, the Local Chief Executive advise the Municipal Accountant to timely prepare and submit all the required reports.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer undertake periodic reconciliation of balances of RPT and SET Receivable accounts per books of accounts with that of the balances per master list of RPT delinquencies to come up with accurate and reconciled RPT and SET Receivable account balances; and
The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments entries depending on the results of validation and reconciliation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Restore the mentioned accounts by making necessary adjusting entries; and
File a request before the COA, following the Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that Management ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees, by withholding or suspension of their salaries;
Prioritize the issuance of demand letters to require immediate liquidation of cash advances of officers and employees who have already died or no longer in service. After exhausting efforts to have these accounts settled and no other alternative procedures are available, management may file a request for write-off through channels in accordance with Section 8 of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
Comply with the requirements set forth under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 which provides for the Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances, and Fund Transfers of National Government Agencies, LGUs and Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations.
Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of receivables and provide allowance for impairment as deemed necessary.
Prepare necessary adjusting entry for the computed impairment of receivables.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to verify undeposited accounts and prepare the relevant journal entries made from for CY 2024 to identify the discrepancies between the mentioned reciprocal accounts. Reconcile and adjust, if necessary, to reflect the correct account balances for fair presentation of accounts in the FS.

We recommended that the LCE ensure that the:
Inventory Committee conduct comprehensive annual physical count and prepare the required RPCPPE, properly reconciled with the records of the GSO and Municipal Accountant every after conduct of physical count and cause the submission within the deadline set;
Municipal Accountant and GSO maintain PPELC and Property Cards for easy reconciliation of PPE accounts; and
Municipal Accountant, after reconciliation of PPE records, to effect necessary adjusting entries for variances noted, if any, to establish the accuracy of the reported balances of the PPE.

We recommended that Management develop and implement a plan of action for the effective utilization of the low-cost housing project in harmony with the objectives of the LGU in constructing the project and considering the government mandate to judiciously utilize funds to prevent further loss.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to ensure that the Inventory Committee:
Prepare corresponding RPCI and submit the same to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with the reported inventory balances recorded per books; and
Prioritize the conduct a complete physical count of all inventories every end of semester or at least once a year and submit the RPCI immediately after the count.
We also recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed, that after reconciliation of Inventory records, the necessary adjusting entries for variances noted, if any, will be made to establish the accuracy of the reported balances of the Inventory accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Take into consideration the imposition of liquidated damages for those projects that meets the conditions as specified in Item 9.1, Annex E of the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184, if warranted (Updated as of 15 April 2023); and
Inform the contractor/supplier to fully implement the project immediately in order for the intended recipients to promptly benefit from the projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to strengthen internal controls on the assessment, collection and monitoring of fees collected for the rental of heavy equipment, as follows:
Follow the enumerated steps as prescribed in the Revenue Code; and
Prepare the ALMHE and identify the specific date when the lessee/renter will use the equipment;
The lessee/renter will pay the rented fees based on the ALMHE; and
The lessee/renter will present the OR and ALMHE to the personnel assigned in monitoring the heavy equipment
Ensure that the personnel assigned in the monitoring and releasing the heavy equipment to maintain a logbook to record the actual time of equipment’s usage as well as the time the lessee/renter returns it.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to relocate the generator to a more secured place while waiting for the construction of an enclosed shed with a roof, to provide adequate protection from environmental exposure and guarantee the proper use, longevity, and efficiency of the equipment.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the HRMO, prioritize the processing of payment of the terminal leave benefits ensuring that all requirements are complete following the minimum documentary requirements enunciated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed to stop granting honorarium to the employees who are not included in the list of specified national government officials entitled to receive honoraria, as provided in DBM Compensation Policy Guidelines No. 98-1.

We recommended that Management:
Intensify Information, Education and Communication (IEC) campaign to disseminate information to the public regarding programs, plans and activities of the LGU pursuant to its Solid Waste Management and to inform and educate them of the provisions in RA No. 9003 and their responsibilities as waste generators;
Install appropriate compartments on the vehicles use for the education and transport of solid wastes to facilities efficient storing and sorted wastes while in transit; and
Strictly enforce the fines and penalties in accordance with Section 49 of RA No. 9003 and Section 14 of Municipal Ordinance No. 16 -s 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the GAD Focal Person prepare and establish the GAD budget by attribution, integrating the GAD perspective in major programs as a means of gradually increasing the allocation on GAD budget and the gender responsiveness of the agency.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC:
Assign a BAC Member to open the first and second envelopes submitted by the bidders;
Ensure the BAC Secretariat prepare a checklist of Eligibility Document Requirements that will be distributed to each BAC Members during opening of bids for easy checking of submitted documents by the bidder; and
Diligently check the documents submitted by the bidders based on the checklist of requirements and specifically put a remark the bidder has PASSED or FAILED the evaluation and have them affix their signature on the checklist.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC Chairman ensure that the BAC Secretariat send written notices to losing bidders notifying the bidding results and the recommendation of award of the contract.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the Municipal Treasurer:

Send communications to the concerned payees or creditors informing them on the status of the checks to be claimed;

If the concerned payees or creditors appeared to claim the checks, issue a new check to the claimant; and

If after exhaustion of communication phase and there are still unclaimed checks, furnish the Municipal Accountant with the original DV and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the JEV following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to verify & record necessary adjusting entries to present the financial statements fairly in accordance with the IPSAS.

Moving forward, require the office of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to have a monitoring mechanism of the age of issued checks to identify if there are any stale checks.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the: 

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconstruct the needed documents to facilitate immediate liquidation and return the savings from completed projects to the source agencies. Henceforth, projects should be fully implemented within the time frame to avoid delay in liquidation;

Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation of on-going projects; and

Municipal Accountant reconciles the amounts between the controlling account, the General Ledger (GL), and the SL, and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries, thereof, to have a fair presentation of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following journal entries for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statement.

Further, the Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to record Interest income earned from deposits under the Trust Fund as Interest Income and recognize both the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds – under General Fund books and Due to Other Funds – under Trust Fund books.  The Municipal Accountant to identify the bank accounts related to the interest and transfer it to the General Fund removing the reciprocal accounts.

Moving forward, Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to study constantly COA accounting rules and regulations and consult with the audit team about the proper implementation whenever possible.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Strictly observe the standards in the preparation of financial statements by including the restated amounts from the application of an accounting policy and disclosing the same in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and Revisit the IPSAS and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of restated Financial Statements.

We recommended and  Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:

Properly disclose the implementation of the above-mentioned resolution along with EO 138, their details, and financial and non-financial effects to the LGU;

Revise the account name of the account “Share from Internal Revenue Allotment” to “Share from National Tax Allotment” in compliance with the above-mentioned resolution and EO; and

Moving forward, to study constantly IFAC publications vis-a-vis COA accounting rules and regulations and consult with the audit team about the proper implementation of the same whenever possible.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to identify the causes of the decreasing collection of local revenues and/or intensify collection efforts by employing various strategies.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct and assure that the: 

Human Resources Management Officer hasten the recruitment and selection process of the remaining unfilled positions; and

Appointed MENRO comply with the qualification standards per ORA – OHRA or for management to appoint a new MENRO who fits the qualification standards.

We recommended and Management agreed to provide the SDO with a safe or steel cabinet exclusively for the CF or IF expenditures. Further, we also recommend that Management require the SDO to maintain a separate CDR and record there all the previously recorded expenditures to ensure proper record keeping.

We recommended and Management agreed to require all the accountable officers to faithfully adhere to the existing rules on the receipt and remittance/deposit of all collections handled by them including the preparation of the required reports, which have penal sanctions. Further for the liquidating officer to prepare the RCDs of the collections previously as document in the unofficial handwritten document in order to properly account the collections.

We recommended and Management also agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to properly monitor all collectors through strict implementation of the rules cited in the NGAS manual for LGUs.

We recommended and Management agreed to implement it’s 10-year SWMP for CY 2022 - 2032 consistent with the National SWM framework and the NSWMC guidebook especially on the estimated income and expected operating and capital expenditures.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the following:

The concerned offices to prioritize the titling of the land to prove claim of ownership and to safeguard government interest and ensure that its land are free from legal issues before facilities are constructed thereon and for the GSO/Legal Office to maintain a record/custody of all the legal documents pertaining to the properties acquired, whether by cash or donation.

The Accounting Office to coordinate with the GSO and Assessor’s office to provide reconciliation amongst their records.

Municipal Accountant derecognized the donated lot in the PPE Account representing transfer of ownership.  A Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) should be prepared by the accounting unit and furnished a copy to COA for reference.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of restated financial statements. Moving forward to the implementation of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, future restatements are to be in accordance with Sections 246 and 247.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the difference between the actual amounts presented in the SCBAA and the amount in the Statement of Financial Performance. Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, to strictly follow the provisions for SCBAA as described in sections 370 – 373 of Volume I.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 2 and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements. Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, to strictly follow the proforma illustrations for the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the reconciliation of liabilities arising from financing activities and its required details. An illustration was provided for by IPSAS 2.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to timely prepare the PMR, for approval by the Head of the Procurement Entity and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and Electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other in order to prevent future delays in computations and preparations of pertinent accounting documents to facilitate the prompt release and to release immediately any unreleased shares to the barangays concerned.

In addition, the LGU shall coordinate with the barangay officials in the observance of the prompt claim of said share from Real Property Tax (RPT) and Community Tax Certificate (CTC) and/or other claims.

We recommended that Local Finance Committee to identify additional sources of income and/or push for the passage of updated revenue code to boost local taxation and design a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue collection based on the current state of the Municipality’s economic enterprises; and 

The Municipal Treasurer to monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the HRMO to thoroughly plan its personnel complement so that they will be appropriately compensate ed in accordance with existing civil service and labor laws, rules and regulations without sacrificing the quality of public service.

We recommended that management:

Trace and provide data for the unexpended prior year’ balances and justify whether the same has been implemented for 20 per cent EDF projects and for the ensuing years to not exclude from the 20 per cent -line item reverted or re-aligned programs and projects from the 20 per cent EDF even if the same be in the nature of an MOOE;

Initiate the needed coordination, monitoring and evaluation on the implementation to assure that all unimplemented projects and programs be given immediate action and implementation. Such that, the execution of the respective responsibilities of the Local Development Council, Executive Committee and of the Local Chief Executive as mandated must be strictly adhered.

Faithfully adhere to the policies and procedures in coming up with a supplemental budget or for any changes in the approved Annual Budget, especially for special funds like the 20 per cent DF, by proper evaluation of the status of PPAs, which will be the basis in reverting programs and projects or to establish savings therefrom as source of appropriation. Also, proper recording and monitoring of the 20 per cent DF reverted or re-aligned; and

Onwards, require the LGU Planning and Development Office to submit a yearly cumulative status of the PPAs, specifically under the 20 per cent DF to facilitate monitoring and evaluation thereof.

We recommended that Management:

Undertake action particularly on the submission of a justification letter for the identified projects with cost discrepancies as these are still subject to reconsideration or appeal to the RTAG COA IX; and

Ensure that the preparation of the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) is compliant with DPWH Department Order No. 68, series of 2016 and other related and applicable laws, rules and regulations to avoid any excess of the Project Cost over COA Cost Estimate.

We recommended that the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the Sangguniang Bayan pass an ordinance localizing RA 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places.

Further, we recommended that the ordinance include provisions on the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended that Management intensify its effort to have an approved 10-year Ecological SWMP by NSWMC to ensure efficient management of solid waste and enhancing ecological balance purposely for the general welfare of the entire constituents.

We recommended that the management immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the buildings owned and so that no fines, penalties, abatements or closures are imposed on the agency.

We recommended that:

Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accounting Office with copies of the report of completed projects and other necessary documents; 

Municipal Treasurer to update and reconcile inventory records; and

Inventory committee to complete the actual physical count on a specified time frame and submit complete physical inventory report in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to consider the asset recognition principle in recording procurements made by the municipality.

Also, direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to properly reclassify the accounts to come up with a more reliable financial statement. The recording of expenditures shall only be made upon issuance of supplies and materials as provided in Section 121 of the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that the LCE instructs the GFPS to include in the Annual GPB the attendance to seminars on Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework (GMEF) and Gender Responsive LGU (GeRL) Assessment Tool to capacitate themselves in the conduct of gender audit.

Further, we recommend that the GFPS conduct gender audit on its agency through the use of GMEF and GeRL Assessment Tool
We recommended that the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the Sangguniang Bayan pass an ordinance localizing RA 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places.

2.Sanggunian, LFC or Economic Development Officer if any, to deliberate and prepare a 5-year Business Plan (BP) especially with the newly established LEEs;

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 are strictly observed to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances by instructing the:
a.) Inventory Committee to assign a unique Property Number for each PPE item following the required code format in the numbering system as provided in Section 5 of the Circular.

b.) Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit the same to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities in compliance with the pertinent provisions of Section 5 of the Circular.

c.) Each office of the LGU to fill up the Inventory Count Form and submit the same to the Property Unit for verification. The form will then be used by the Inventory Committee as basis in the preparation of its PIP and will be considered as part of their inventory working paper.
d.) Inventory Committee to record/document the physical count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major account group and will be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE after the physical count.

e.) Property Unit to  prepare a List of PPEs Found at Station (Annex B of the Circular) and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) during physical inventory and submit these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive improve the internal control system of the LGU by addressing the gaps presented
1.Correct the internal issues presented by the computer system, and make a patch to improve the already existing system
2.Request that continuous customer support be easily available whenever issues were presented;

We recommended that management intensify its effort to have an approved 10-year Ecological SWMP by NSWMC to ensure efficient management of solid waste and enhancing ecological balance purposely for the general welfare of the entire constituents.

We recommended that the municipality close the dump site or convert it to an eco-park to avoid health and environmental hazards and to avoid penal sanctions as provided by RA 9003. We recommended that the LCE instructs the GFPS to include in the Annual GPB the attendance to seminars on Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework (GMEF) and Gender Responsive LGU (GeRL) Assessment Tool to capacitate themselves in the conduct of gender audit.

We recommended that the LGU create and establish Monitoring and Evaluation Team in accordance with the provision Section 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to evaluate the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects.
We recommended that management direct the GFPS to conduct continuous monitoring on the implementation of GAD PAPs identified in the GAD Plan and Budget.
We recommended that the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the Sangguniang Bayan pass an ordinance localizing RA 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places.

We recommended that the municipality:
Create a RC for the GAD Focal Point System; and
Regularly visit the COA website to be updated with the all the recent COA issuances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to revise the Four-Day compressed work week in order to properly provide the required one hour lunch break. Further, for the month of July, charge against all the VL days of all the Municipal Employees. We recommended that management immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the buildings owned and so that no fines, penalties, abatements or closures are imposed on the agency.

2.	Stop the practice of paying from the SEF expenses which are not allowable; and
3.	Include allocations from their SEF for the ECCD PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Demand Letters be issued to the erring officials and employees of the municipality but this time a reasonable liquidation period be set whereby the appropriate charges shall then be filed if left unattended pursuant to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code. Limit the granting of cash advances to those provided for in Section 3 of COA Cir. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer, initiate the conduct of physical inventory taking of all properties of the municipal government and prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the audit team as basis to determine the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balance in the financial statements.

We recommend that the management should establish internal control policies and procedures over Property and Supply Management system to:
Safeguard the accuracy and reliability of the information by preventing and detecting errors;
Prevent or detect loss of assets and records on a timely basis;
Protect against waste in all aspects of operation and discourage other types of inefficient use of resources; and
Provide reasonable assurance that those procedures and rules are adhered to by the agency personnel.

We recommend that the management to provide adequate and safe shelter for storage of supplies, parking space of the vehicle/equipment to safeguard and to prolong the useful life of the assets.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to thoroughly review past transactions and the journal entries made on the reciprocal accounts in order that the necessary adjustments be made.

We recommend the Municipal Accountants to confirm, validate, and review the accounts and to effect the necessary adjustments thereof. Request authority from the source agency to  utilize the unexpended balance before the same can be transferred to the General Fund should there be no provision for such in the Memorandum of Agreement or guidelines in the utilization of the funds.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the 2017 GAA pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough to support their daily needs until the next payday. It is informed that effective CY 2018, the employee’s monthly net take home pay shall not be lower than P 5,000.00 as provided in the 2018 GAA.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to comply with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 on the preparation of BRS to achieve an accurate financial reporting of Cash in Bank account and reconcile with the Bank Reconciliation with the General Ledgers.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to require the personnel concerned in the Accounting and Budget to use the proper account codes as prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units (RCALGU) to have a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the LGU officers and employees conduct gender analysis and assessment in order to formulate policies and plans which can address the relevant gender issues in the locality and mainstream the programs, projects and activities that could solve the issues identified to attain gender equality, monitor its implementation and report on its results to ensure that the different concerns of women and men are addressed equally and equitably in their PAPs in accordance with the provisions of PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular 2013-01 and as amended by Joint Circular 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.

We recommend that Demand Letters be again issued to the erring officials and employees of the municipality but this time a reasonable liquidation period be set whereby  appropriate charges shall then be filed if left unattended pursuant to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code. Civil actions to be initiated to run after their properties. For those who are still in the active service the corresponding withholding of their benefits be affected for the un-liquidated cash advances.

Limit the granting of cash advances to those provided for in Section 3 of COA Circular. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should analyzed thoroughly the reconciling items and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect  the accurate balance of the cash-in bank account pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Acting Municipal Accountant and other Officials concerned should insure all insurable risks to protect the interest of the local government. Also, require the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the budget funds for premium payments of properties to be insured with GSIS.

We recommend the procurement of common-use supplies should be made through public bidding pursuant to the provisions of RA 9184 unless condition exist that will justify its procurement through the alternative mode.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommend that Demand Letters be again issued to the erring officials and employees of the municipality but this time a reasonable liquidation period be set whereby  appropriate charges shall then be filed if left unattended pursuant to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code. Civil actions to be initiated to run after their properties. For those who are still in the active service the corresponding withholding of their benefits be affected for the un-liquidated cash advances.
Limit the granting of cash advances to those provided for in Section 3 of COA Circular. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should strictly observe the guidelines on the preparation and presentation of the Financial Statements specifically on the submission of a complete sets of financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile monthly their respective records to avoid the accumulation of the reconciling items and to facilitate the reconciliation of their balances.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and other Officials concerned to insure government properties with the GSIS against any insurable risk.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to stamp “PAID” all disbursement vouchers/payrolls and its supporting documents upon payment to avoid reusing of the same for another payment.

Recommendations:
We recommend that management to observe the following:
Submission of contracts, purchase order, loan agreements and other agreements entered into by the LGU to the auditor within five (5) days upon perfection thereof pursuant to Section 6.6 of COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 2009-002; and
Notify the auditor of deliveries of goods and services procured by the agency within twenty four (24) hours after acceptance pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the management to instruct the concerned officials to submit the paid disbursement vouchers including the supporting documents to the audit team for examination and verification in compliance with the above cited regulation.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the management to instruct the concerned officials to submit the Program of Works including the supporting documents to the audit team for review in compliance with the above cited regulation.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the official drivers of the Local Government and other concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. The Monthly reports will provide a summary of trips per vehicles in chronological order of the serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets.

Recommendation:
We recommend to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to examine the completeness of the supporting documents for travel before payment.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks. Accordingly, to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) debiting the Cash-in-bank, LCCA account and crediting Accounts Payable to reflect the accurate balances of both the cash-in-bank and accounts payable accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to adopt internal control measures to preclude the incurrence of unnecessary expenditures in the form of bank services charges and to ensure that:
Issuances of checks are duly backed up with cash in the depository bank; and
Errors, such as, differences in the amounts indicated in the check and Advice of Checks Issued, be avoided.
Proper controls and procedures in the payment of obligations and in the monitoring of the respective cash in bank balances should be observed to avoid possible disallowance in post-audit.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the LGU officers and employees to conduct gender analysis and assessment in accordance with the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-1 in order to formulate policies and plans which can address the relevant gender issues in the locality and mainstream the programs, projects and activities that could solve the issues identified to attain gender equality.
It is recommended that the amount of P3,590,509.41 be adjusted to the proper asset account by drawing a Journal Voucher (JEV).
